version: 1
name: {{workflow-name}}
status: draft
owner: {{workflow-name}}
parent: {{group-name}}
owns: []
external-systems: []
---

# {{Workflow Name}}

<!-- Workflow files are arboretum's L3 components (per the C4-derived hierarchy
     in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §4). They double as governance specs in
     workflow-mode (D17): every owned file declares `# owner: {{workflow-name}}`,
     and `/consolidate` reconciles workflow content with its owned code.

     Sections are classified by authorship:
       HUMAN        — written by the human; never overwritten by /consolidate.
       AUTO         — regenerated by /consolidate from current code on each pass.
       APPEND-AUTO  — /consolidate appends new entries; existing rows preserved.

     Frontmatter notes:
       owner       — workflow files are self-owning (forward declaration per D18).
       parent      — the group this workflow belongs to. Omit if the workflow has
                     no parent group (Reading 1) or in projects without groups.
       owns        — files governed by this workflow (each declares `# owner:`
                     pointing back here).
       external-systems — non-arboretum dependencies (e.g., gh, github-projects).
                     Optional. Omit or leave [] if none. -->

## Job (JTBD)

<!-- HUMAN — Required. Canonical when/want/so form. What is this workflow hired
     to do, in the user's voice?

     Example:
     > When I'm a domain expert at sprint-end with recent work fresh in mind,
     > I want a structured pass that captures lessons, tidies docs, and audits
     > what may have drifted, so the project stays healthy and my domain
     > knowledge accumulates instead of evaporating. -->

## Boundaries (non-goals)

<!-- HUMAN — Required. What this workflow is NOT. Explicit scope contract
     preventing creep over time. Bullet list of "Does NOT ..." statements.
     Each non-goal redirects the user to the workflow that DOES handle it.

     Example:
     - Does NOT make architectural changes (use `architecture` or `build`).
     - Does NOT decide what's in the next sprint (use `pre-sprint planning`).
     - Does NOT *fix* problems found during audit (the fix becomes its own
       `bug-fix` or `chore` workflow instance). -->

## Inputs

<!-- HUMAN — Required. What this workflow needs to start. Bullet list.
     Be specific about source — files, GitHub state, prior workflow outputs. -->

## Outputs

<!-- HUMAN — Required. What this workflow produces. Bullet list.
     If outputs are consumed by another workflow, name the consumer. -->

## Sequence

<!-- HUMAN — Required. Basic flow of work, in order. Numbered list.
     Each step is one phase of the workflow. Phases call skills (named
     in the Skills used section). Keep phases at the level of "what"
     not "how" — implementation details belong in the skill files themselves. -->

## Skills used

<!-- HUMAN — Required. Internal tools (skills) the workflow hires.
     Distinguish primary skills (owned by this workflow per D20) from
     consumed skills (owned elsewhere; called from here).

     Format:
       `/skill-name` (primary | consumed) — what the skill contributes here.

     Example:
       - `/maintain` (primary) — orchestrates the doc tidy / audit / learning steps.
       - `/health-check` (consumed; primary owner: `architecture`) — runs the
         audit step. -->

## Cadence

<!-- HUMAN — Optional. Include only when non-trivial.
     Often inferable from the workflow's lifecycle category (foundational /
     continuous / sprint / ad-hoc / occasional). Worth declaring when there's
     a specific trigger or timing constraint. -->

## External systems

<!-- HUMAN — Optional. Mirror the frontmatter `external-systems:` list with
     one-line descriptions of how each is used. Skip if none or if usage is
     self-evident from the Sequence. -->

## Tests

<!-- AUTO — Regenerated by /consolidate from test files owned by this workflow.
     Lists each test by name with tier (unit / contract / integration / domain).
     Declare "N/A — [reason]" for inapplicable tiers. -->

## Implementation Notes

<!-- HUMAN (free text) + AUTO (Design record subsection below).
     File locations, constraints, and guidance for implementation. -->

### Design record

<!-- AUTO — Citation list of design specs and plans referenced by this
     workflow's history. One line per artifact. No embedded design content.

     Example:
       - `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-29-strategy-and-jobs-design.md` — JTBD
         framing and lifecycle workflow model.
       - `docs/plans/2026-05-15-maintain-skill-rename.md` — implementation plan
         for renaming /reflect to /maintain. -->

## Decisions

<!-- APPEND-AUTO — /consolidate harvests new rows from design specs and plans.
     Existing rows are never modified. The Source column is the idempotency
     key — /consolidate does not re-add a decision whose source artifact +
     decision ID is already cited. -->

| ID | Decision | Alternatives Considered | Rationale | Date | Source |
|----|----------|------------------------|-----------|------|--------|
